summary: McKenzie announces a revamped public stats tracking page for Bingo Card Creator, consolidating all business metrics into one location. While working on 2008 taxes, he provides a detailed financial breakdown showing $21,117 in sales, $11,783 in expenses, and $9,334 in profit (roughly 45% margin). Advertising, primarily Google AdWords, was his largest expense category. He notes that pre-2008 expense data is incomplete due to difficulty accessing old credit card statements.
key_points: Bingo Card Creator generated ~$21K revenue and ~$9.3K profit in 2008, a 45% profit margin
Google AdWords was the overwhelming majority of his advertising spend, his single largest expense category
He practices radical transparency by making all business stats publicly available at a single URL
Tax preparation forced a useful discipline of properly tracking and categorizing all expenses
money_quotes: The big green slice is profit (~45% — a bit lower than I expected).
Unsurprisingly, my number one cost is advertising — the overwhelming majority of it is Google AdWords.
I don't have easy access to all my old 2006/2007 credit card statements, and don't even get me started about Paypal.
key_insight: Radical financial transparency — publishing exact revenue, expenses, and profit margins — builds trust and credibility with an audience of fellow software entrepreneurs.
